rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive coupling of unactivated olefins with ketones to form tertiary homoallylic/saturated alcohols via cathodically generated ketyl radicals at a Sn cathode with a sacrificial Zn anode
electrochemical_role: Cathodic one-electron reduction of the ketone at a Sn cathode generates a strongly surface-adsorbed ketyl radical that adds to the olefin; a second cathodic reduction of the resulting C-radical to a carbanion is enabled by Zn2+ (from sacrificial Zn anode) acting as a thermodynamic sink and via ZnBr2 coordination to the ketone

key_intermediates.name: β-hydroxy carbon-centered radical (after addition to olefin)

electron_flow: At the Sn cathode the ketone undergoes one-electron reduction to a strongly adsorbed ketyl radical (E), which adds to the unactivated olefin (C); a second one-electron reduction of the resulting C-radical (second E), facilitated by Zn2+ from the sacrificial Zn anode, yields a carbanion that is protonated on workup (second C) — an overall ECEC sequence.
rate_determining_step: Electrochemical reduction of the ketone (current-limited; kinetics zero-order in all components except current)

probe_reagents.name: α-substituted ketone 60 (R = H or TBS)
probe_reagents.observation: Elimination of the α-substituent observed (product 62, 48%), consistent with a ketyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Allyl alcohol 64
probe_reagents.observation: Bis-addition adduct 65 (50%) formed, suggesting carbanion intermediate where ZnBr2 assists departure of OH and regenerates an olefin

probe_reagents.name: Acetone-d6
probe_reagents.observation: 80% D incorporation at the highlighted position of product 67-d (74%), supporting a carbanion intermediate protonated on workup

probe_reagents.name: d7-DMF (with non-deuterated acetone)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% D incorporation in product 68 (72%), ruling out DMF as the proton source

probe_reagents.name: Kinetic order analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Zero-order in all components except current — reduction is purely electrochemical/current-limited

probe_reagents.name: Acetophenone (CV/SWV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Pre-peaks observed on Sn (not on GC) indicate strong adsorption of ketyl radical to Sn; addition of olefin 61 causes anodic shift in cathodic peak (EC behavior); SWV with ZnBr2 reveals three reduction peaks, the third assigned to ZnBr2-coordinated ketone, supporting ECEC

notes: The Sn cathode is critical: strong adsorption of the ketyl radical (evidenced by CV pre-peaks absent on GC) modulates its reactivity, enabling coupling with unactivated olefins rather than typical nucleophilic ketyl behavior. The role of the sacrificial Zn anode is dual: avoids competing anodic oxidation and supplies Zn2+/ZnBr2 that coordinates the ketone, lowering the second reduction potential to make the ECEC pathway feasible. Limitation: α-substituted ketones undergo elimination rather than productive coupling.
